threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Skip manual verdicts when enforcing strict backfill**

Strict mode currently fails whenever `backfill_needed` is non-empty, even after `merge_manual_overrides` has promoted those same threads to `verdict_source:
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. After `merge_with_existing` promotes a thread to verdict_source=manual, the enforcement loop now filters it out of `backfill_needed` so strict mode no longer fails on confirmed dispositions. New test: test_manual_source_excluded_from_backfill.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Honor configured reviewer allowlist in enforcement**

Enforcement adds every resolved thread without a parsed block to `backfill_needed` regardless of `cfg.reviewers`, so `warning/strict` behavior is applied to a
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. Enforcement now consults `cfg.is_tracked_reviewer(rec.reviewer)` before appending to BACKFILL/RESOLVE NEEDED. Untracked reviewers (humans, bots not in the configured list) no longer trigger violations. New test: test_reviewers_list_scopes_enforcement.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Use GH_REPO fallback before rejecting missing --repo**

The CLI help says `--repo` defaults to `GH_REPO`, but sync exits with an error whenever `--repo` is omitted. In environments that set `GH_REPO` (and in the
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. `_sync_core` now reads `os.environ.get('GH_REPO')` as a fallback when --repo is omitted, matching `gh`'s convention. Help text updated. New test: test_gh_repo_env_fallback.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Paginate thread comments before parsing verdict blocks**

The GraphQL query fetches `comments(first:50)` for each thread but never paginates that nested connection, so replies after the 50th comment are silently
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. The GraphQL query now bumps the initial page to first:100 AND requests pageInfo on the comments connection. A new `_paginate_thread_comments` helper performs follow-up fetches for any thread that exceeds 100 comments. New test: test_comments_pagination_over_50 (parser-side; full fetch path validated via the offline mode).

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Avoid inferring OBSOLETE without a commit reference**

The default `obsolete-bare` inference rule emits `verdict: OBSOLETE` without setting `commit`, but the tool’s own schema rules require a commit for `OBSOLETE
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. The `obsolete-bare` rule is removed from DEFAULT_INFERENCE_RULES. Inferred OBSOLETE entries without a commit would have failed `validate`; the richer `already-addressed-with-commit` rule still catches legitimate OBSOLETE-with-commit cases. New test: test_obsolete_bare_rule_emits_valid_record.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Handle invalid capture-group indexes in inference rules**

Inference rule execution trusts `commit_group`/`ref_group` values from config and directly calls `m.group(...)`; if the regex matches but does not define
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. The `m.group(commit_group/ref_group)` calls are now wrapped in try/except for IndexError, re.error, and TypeError. A misconfigured rule logs a warning to stderr and is skipped; inference continues with the remaining rules. New test: test_inference_rule_bad_capture_group_skipped.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Catch invalid verdict blocks instead of crashing sync**

When any reply contains a malformed `review-verdict` block (for example an unknown verdict value), `parse_all_blocks` raises `BlockValidationError` and the
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. `extract_blocks_from_thread` now wraps `parse_all_blocks` in try/except BlockValidationError, logs a warning naming the offending thread+comment, and continues processing. One typo no longer aborts the whole sync. New test: test_malformed_block_in_reply_doesnt_crash_sync.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Match applies_to_reviewer against canonical profile login**

Reviewer-scoped inference rules do not fire for aliased logins because the check compares `applies_to_reviewer` only to the observed login and the alia
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. Added `Config.canonical_login_for()` and threaded canonical-login through `run_inference_rules`. A rule's `applies_to_reviewer` now matches when the observed login, the canonical-login it resolves to, OR any of the profile's aliases matches. New test: test_reviewer_scoped_rule_honors_alias.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Restrict obsolete keyword inference to reply comments**

The `obsolete-bare` rule scans `all_bodies`, so it can infer `OBSOLETE` from the reviewer’s original finding text (for example, a finding that merely menti
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. The bare-word rules (`deferred-bare`, `duplicate-bare`) and the deferred-per-{ADR,U-log,version} rules are now scoped to `reply_only`. A reviewer's original finding text mentioning 'duplicate concern' or 'deferred per ADR-X' no longer triggers inference; only maintainer replies do. New test: test_bare_rules_scoped_to_reply_only.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Filter enforcement to configured reviewer list**

Only `cfg.reviewers` should drive policy violations, but the `BACKFILL NEEDED` path appends every resolved thread without a verdict block regardless of author. In
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed; same change as PRRT_kwDOSjmLjM6EPXgP. Both BACKFILL NEEDED and RESOLVE NEEDED paths now gate on `cfg.is_tracked_reviewer(rec.reviewer)`. New test: test_reviewers_list_scopes_enforcement.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Honor GH_REPO fallback when --repo is omitted**

The CLI help advertises that `--repo` defaults from `GH_REPO`, but sync immediately errors when `--repo` is missing instead of checking the environment. This makes
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed; same change as PRRT_kwDOSjmLjM6EPXgR. `os.environ.get('GH_REPO')` is consulted when --repo is unset. New test: test_gh_repo_env_fallback.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Paginate thread comments before extracting verdict blocks**

The GraphQL query fetches only `comments(first:50)` for each thread and does not paginate comment pages. On long-lived threads with more than 50 commen
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed; same change as PRRT_kwDOSjmLjM6EPXgU. The comments connection now uses first:100 and paginates via `_paginate_thread_comments`. New test: test_comments_pagination_over_50.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Match inference rules against replies, not reviewer findings**

Several default inference rules are scoped to `all_bodies`, so terms like "deferred" in the original reviewer finding can trigger an inferred verdic
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed; same change as PRRT_kwDOSjmLjM6EPXgj. The bare-word and the deferred-per-* rules are now reply_only. New test: test_bare_rules_scoped_to_reply_only.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Handle invalid capture-group indexes in inference rules**

The inference engine tolerates invalid regex patterns and verdict values, but it directly dereferences `m.group(commit_group/ref_group)` without guarding
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed; same change as PRRT_kwDOSjmLjM6EPXgZ. `m.group()` calls wrapped in try/except. New test: test_inference_rule_bad_capture_group_skipped.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Restrict @codex review trigger to trusted commenters**

The `issue_comment` path currently runs whenever any PR comment contains `@codex review`, but it does not verify `github.event.comment.author_association` (
threads.verdict_notes: REJECTED — the file `.github/workflows/codex-review.yml` was reverted in d35af34 (this PR no longer ships the codex-action workflow; see commit message for the API-billing rationale). The underlying concern (gating `@codex review` triggers on `author_association` to prevent untrusted commenters running paid Codex jobs) IS valid and should be added if/when codex-action is re-introduced as a fallback for Codex Cloud env-disconnect scenarios.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Apply reconsidered verdict as the effective thread verdict**

When a `review-verdict-reconsidered` block is present, sync records it in `reconsidered_verdict` but leaves `verdict`/`verdict_commit`/`verdict_notes`
threads.verdict_notes: Fixed. When sync sees a `review-verdict-reconsidered` block, the record's verdict/commit/notes are now the RECONSIDERED values; the original is pushed into `verdict_history` so the chain of custody is preserved. New test: test_reconsidered_supersedes_current_verdict.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Guard template Codex job when secrets are unavailable**

The install template runs on all non-draft `pull_request` events while requiring `secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY`; for forked/Dependabot PR contexts those secrets
threads.verdict_notes: REJECTED — install template reverted in d35af34. The concern about secret guards in the template (so downstream adopters don't inherit deterministic failures on fork PRs) is valid and should be incorporated when the template returns.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Skip Codex job for fork/Dependabot PRs without secrets**

The job runs for every non-draft `pull_request`, but `openai-api-key` comes from `secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY`; GitHub does not pass repository secrets to work
threads.verdict_notes: REJECTED — file reverted in d35af34, as above. The underlying concern (fork/Dependabot PRs failing deterministically due to missing OPENAI_API_KEY) IS valid and should be addressed via an `if: ` guard on secret presence if/when codex-action returns.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Restrict template @codex trigger to trusted commenters**

The installable workflow template has the same `issue_comment` trigger condition but no `author_association` gate, so any outside commenter can invoke exp
threads.verdict_notes: REJECTED — `tools/review-journal/install/codex-action-review.yml` was reverted in d35af34. The concern about trusted-commenter gating in the install template is valid and should be incorporated if the template returns; recorded for the next time.
